rules: Every new chat starts UNAUTHENTICATED regardless of activeMember in state.
activeMember is only a hint of the last user, never proof of authentication.
A valid session requires a successful login in the current conversation.
After idleTimeoutMinutes of inactivity the session expires and login is required again.
Before any GitHub commit/push or identity-bound action, the authenticated session member must match the actor.
Every authenticated user-facing answer must start with the active named agent line.
If no active named agent exists, ask the user to name the required agent before answering the task.
Party Mode keeps its dialogue format, but the final handoff to the user must still show the speaking agent.
Save one-line memories to a monthly JSONL file so new chats can restore context.
Generate SESSION_BRIEF.md from recent monthly memory entries.
Read memory brief before answering any authenticated task.
Route every authenticated conversation through one primary named agent.
For new systems, unclear product ideas, or multi-step builds, route first to a high-reasoning architect/planner agent.
If the task is ambiguous, ask concise clarifying questions before implementation.
Do not activate irrelevant agents, but there is no maximum support-agent limit when the task genuinely needs more expertise.
Keep the user's original intent visible in the route reason so the agent does not drift from context.
Party mode activates automatically when a task needs several specialist agents to reason together.
When Party mode is active, show PARTY MODE in large uppercase before the agent dialogue.
Normal Party mode should use at least 3 participants and prefer 4 when the task has enough relevant roles.
The primary agent coordinates the party and keeps the user intent visible.
Every visible agent message must use the configured dialogue format.
Agents should ask each other focused questions, answer directly, then hand a clear result to the user.
Explicit requests such as party mode, multi-agent, semua agent, or agent discussion must activate Party Mode.
If Party Mode needs unnamed agents, ask for all missing agent names before doing the task.
Before browser, scraping, extraction, click automation, login flow, or form filling, run browser preflight.
Check the user's default browser automatically when possible.
If the default browser is Edge, Brave, or Opera, use chrome-direct mode and guide the user to start remote debugging.
If no supported browser is detected, ask the user which browser they use before continuing.

phases.title: Idea Capture
phases.goal: Clarify the user's intent, outcome, audience, constraints, and unknowns.

phases.title: Product Brief
phases.goal: Turn the idea into a concise product direction and first scope.

phases.title: PRD
phases.goal: Define users, requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, and risks.

phases.title: UX Spec
phases.goal: Define flows, states, layout behavior, visual references, and accessibility expectations.

phases.title: Architecture
phases.goal: Choose the system shape, data contracts, boundaries, dependencies, and deployment path.

phases.title: Implementation Story
phases.goal: Break approved scope into implementable tasks with acceptance checks.

phases.title: Readiness Gate
phases.goal: Confirm the work is clear enough to code without guessing.

phases.title: Implementation
phases.goal: Build the approved story with small safe changes and autosycn.

phases.title: Review
phases.goal: Find defects, regressions, missing tests, UX mismatch, and security/performance risks.

phases.title: Release
phases.goal: Prepare deploy, handoff, changelog, and merge/release confirmation.

rules: Defaults define safe project behavior.
Team config may override workflow preferences and agent policy.
Member config may override language, preferred agents, and UI verbosity.
Customization must not bypass auth, agent gate, autosycn identity, or BURHAN-MOP review.

providerParity.rule: All providers use MOP auth, memory, agent routing, workflow, and bridged skill inventory before provider-specific runtime behavior.

rules: MOP Flow is the user-facing brand.
Ruflo and Claude Flow are upstream runtime compatibility names only.
Bridge .claude/skills through the MOP Flow manifest for non-Claude providers.
Do not let provider-specific tools bypass MOP gates.

projectRootPolicy.rootPathMeaning: The current workspace root is the app/project root.
rules: When building or scaffolding a user project, create files directly in the workspace root.
Do not create a nested folder named after the app, product, or project and place the implementation there.
Use normal top-level app folders such as src, app, pages, components, public, assets, tests, docs, config, and scripts.
If a framework command wants to create a new project directory, use the current-directory mode or move generated files to root before continuing.
Ask for explicit permission only when the user truly wants a monorepo or multiple apps.

adversarialReview.name: Adversarial Review
rules: Challenge assumptions without dismissing the user's goal.
Find failure modes, edge cases, hidden scope, and missing tests.
Propose fixes or safer alternatives for each serious finding.
Block implementation when requirements are too unclear.
Member commits must use the active member GitHub account, not an AI or guardian identity.
When gh is authenticated, derive member commit email as ID+USERNAME@users.noreply.github.com.
Refuse autosycn if the active gh account does not match the member githubUsername.
BURHAN-MOP identity is reserved for merge guardian commits only.

installedSkills.name: mop-flow
installedSkills.purpose: Provider-neutral skill bridge and runtime wrapper. Makes MOP the canonical layer above Ruflo / Claude Flow across Claude, Codex, Gemini, and Antigravity.

installedSkills.name: autosycn
installedSkills.purpose: Identity-safe memory + git autosync flow. Commits and merges as the active user, never as the AI tool.

installedSkills.name: auto-deploy
installedSkills.purpose: Opt-in deployment setup for GitHub Pages or Actions, Docker, and Vercel. Must ask before activation.

installedSkills.name: mop-help
installedSkills.purpose: Workflow-aware help skill. Answers what to do next, which agent should lead, and which artifact or gate applies.

agentCatalog.title: Core Agent
agentCatalog.purpose: Default high-context conversation owner and final coordinator.

agentCatalog.title: Product Planner
agentCatalog.purpose: Clarifies ideas, scopes products, asks questions, and turns vague requests into workable plans.

agentCatalog.title: Researcher
agentCatalog.purpose: Finds requirements, references, constraints, and missing context.

agentCatalog.title: System Architect
agentCatalog.purpose: High-reasoning system design, architecture, tradeoffs, and context control.

agentCatalog.title: Coder
agentCatalog.purpose: General code implementation after requirements are clear.

agentCatalog.title: Frontend Developer
agentCatalog.purpose: UI, React, Next.js, styling, state, responsive behavior, and browser UX.

agentCatalog.title: Backend Developer
agentCatalog.purpose: APIs, server logic, authentication, integrations, and business rules.

agentCatalog.title: Database Engineer
agentCatalog.purpose: Schemas, migrations, queries, indexes, and data lifecycle.

agentCatalog.title: Mobile Developer
agentCatalog.purpose: iOS, Android, React Native, Flutter, PWA, offline workflows.

agentCatalog.title: Tester
agentCatalog.purpose: Test strategy, unit/integration/e2e coverage, regression checks, and bug reproduction.

agentCatalog.title: Reviewer
agentCatalog.purpose: Code review, risk review, maintainability, and final quality gate.

agentCatalog.title: Security Specialist
agentCatalog.purpose: Threat modelling, auth, secrets, permissions, dependency risk, and hardening.

agentCatalog.title: Performance Engineer
agentCatalog.purpose: Speed, memory, bundle size, database/query performance, and profiling.

agentCatalog.title: GitHub Coordinator
agentCatalog.purpose: GitHub repo, branch, PR, issue, release, and merge workflow.

agentCatalog.title: Browser Automation Agent
agentCatalog.purpose: Browser preflight, scraping, rendered extraction, clicking, form filling, login flows, and browser-act coordination.

agentCatalog.title: DevOps Engineer
agentCatalog.purpose: CI/CD, Docker, infrastructure, environment config, and deployment pipeline.

agentCatalog.title: Deployment Specialist
agentCatalog.purpose: GitHub Pages/Actions, Docker deployment, Vercel setup, and release handoff.

agentCatalog.title: Memory Keeper
agentCatalog.purpose: Save, recall, summarize, search, and clean long-term MOP records.

agentCatalog.title: UX Researcher
agentCatalog.purpose: User flows, pain points, personas, usability, and product behavior.

agentCatalog.title: Accessibility Specialist
agentCatalog.purpose: Keyboard flow, screen reader behavior, contrast, reduced motion, and inclusive interaction checks.

agentCatalog.title: Documentation Writer
agentCatalog.purpose: README, guides, prompts, specs, changelogs, and concise user-facing docs.

agentCatalog.title: Prompt Engineer
agentCatalog.purpose: Prompt systems, AI workflow design, context discipline, and model behavior.

agentCatalog.title: SEO Strategist
agentCatalog.purpose: Technical SEO, metadata, schema, content structure, and search readiness.

agentCatalog.title: Project Manager
agentCatalog.purpose: Sprint planning, roadmap, task breakdown, deadlines, and stakeholder alignment.

agentCatalog.title: Questmaster
agentCatalog.purpose: Quest design, XP systems, phase gates, progress discipline, and blockers.

agentCatalog.title: Onboarding Steward
agentCatalog.purpose: Setup, roster, invites, authentication, and access-control.

agentCatalog.title: Integrity Guardian
agentCatalog.purpose: Preflight checks, schema validation, consistency checks, and risk analysis.

agentCatalog.title: Recall Scout
agentCatalog.purpose: History search, ledger queries, context restoration, and handoff lookup.

agentCatalog.title: QA Engineer
agentCatalog.purpose: Test plans, coverage gaps, regression checking, and automation script validation.

agentCatalog.title: Prompt Alchemist
agentCatalog.purpose: Prompt engineering, agent protocols, context discipline, and model performance tuning.

agentCatalog.title: Data Analyst
agentCatalog.purpose: Data analysis, performance metrics compilation, trends analysis, and reporting.

agentCatalog.title: Task Orchestrator
agentCatalog.description: Decomposes complex tasks, assigns to agents, tracks dependencies, synthesizes results. The air traffic controller for multi-agent sessions.
agentCatalog.superpower: GOAP planning + dependency graph + result synthesis

agentCatalog.title: Data & ML Engineer
agentCatalog.description: Machine learning model development, training pipelines, evaluation metrics, and deployment config.
agentCatalog.superpower: ML pipeline + data preprocessing + model evaluation

agentCatalog.title: Migration Planner
agentCatalog.description: Database schema migration, API version upgrades, system rewrites. Compatibility matrix + rollback plan.
agentCatalog.superpower: Phased rollout + compatibility matrix + rollback procedure

agentCatalog.title: Sandbox & Environment
agentCatalog.description: Isolated execution environment for testing. Create, configure, teardown. Safe code execution.
agentCatalog.superpower: Environment isolation + lifecycle management + resource monitoring

agentCatalog.title: Workflow & CI Integrator
agentCatalog.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline creation, event triggers, job dependencies, artifact caching.
agentCatalog.superpower: Pipeline generation + matrix testing + workflow automation

agentCatalog.title: Content & Docs Strategist
agentCatalog.description: Technical documentation, API docs (OpenAPI), changelog auto-draft from ledger, onboarding guides.
agentCatalog.superpower: BMAD Paige persona + OpenAPI doc gen + ledger-to-changelog
